title: Remove dead schema fields
description:
**Objective:** Remove unused schema fields from `OcBlackbytesConfigSchema` that have no consumers.

**Context:** The schema at `config/schema/oc-blackbytes-config.ts:44-57` includes fields that were never implemented: `disabled_hooks` (line 48), `mcp_env_alllowlist` (line 50, also a typo), `auto_update` (line 53), `_migrations` (line 57).

**Scope:**
- IN: Verify schema uses non-strict parsing (`.passthrough()` or Zod default behavior) so removing fields doesn't break users who have them
- IN: Remove the 4 dead fields from the schema
- IN: Remove corresponding type references if any
- OUT: Removing fields that ARE consumed (like `disabled_mcps`, `hashline_edit`, etc.)

**Components Touched:**
- `src/config/schema/oc-blackbytes-config.ts` — remove 4 field definitions

**Dependencies:** None

**Assumptions and Constraints:**
- Zod v4 `z.object()` by default strips unknown fields — verify this. If strict, need to add `.passthrough()` or `.strip()` to avoid breaking users with these fields in config

**Risks/Gotchas:**

**Validation:**

title: EPIC: Full unit + E2E test coverage (no mocks)
description:
Achieve comprehensive unit and E2E integration test coverage across the oc-blackbytes plugin without mocks, fakes, stubs, or spies. Use real filesystem temp dirs, real spawned processes, and real plugin bootstrapping. Existing discipline: tests use bun:test with temp dirs and OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR env override for isolation — extend this pattern.

Current state: 38 tests, 6 files, ~87 source files. Major gaps: hashline-edit (17 files, 0 tests), ast-grep, grep, glob, shared tool helpers, MCP factories, most agent/handler code, bootstrap wiring, setup-models command, and any E2E pipeline.

Success = every production source file has direct or compositional coverage; E2E runners exercise the real plugin against a temp OpenCode config dir with greeting banners and detailed step-level logging; CI enforces a coverage threshold.

comments.author: cuongnt3
comments.text:
## Execution order (dependency spine)

1. **EPIC A** (harness) — unblocks everything. Must land first. 6 leaves.
2. **EPICS B–L** (unit tests) — all depend on A; largely parallel. Recommended priority within:
   - First: E (hashline-edit, 17 files untested, most leverage). Leaves E1/E2/E3/E4/E5/E8/E9/E14 are P0.
   - Then: L4 (tool-execute-after read path, P0), I2 (spawn, P0).
   - Then: everything else, P1.
3. **EPIC M** (E2E harness) — depends on A3 (plugin harness) + A5 (logger) + A6 (fixtures). Can start in parallel with unit-test epics once A is done.
4. **EPIC N** (E2E scenarios) — depends on M1. N5 (hashline roundtrip) is P0 because it catches integration regressions across the most complex subsystem.
5. **EPIC O** (coverage gates + CI) — runs last; O2 depends on M1.

## Parallelism sketch
After A: fire B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I, J, K, L simultaneously (disjoint file targets).
After M: fire all N scenarios in parallel (disjoint scenario files).

## Discipline (no-mock policy)
- **No** jest/vitest-style mocks, spies, stubs, fakes.
- Real temp dirs, real spawned processes, real local HTTP server for download tests (I4).
- Binary-dependent tests (sg, rg, find) detect availability via A4 and skip cleanly.
- Test-doubles that ARE allowed: concrete classes with deterministic behavior (e.g., TestClient in A3) — these are real implementations, not mocks. Document this in the file header.

title: A. Test harness foundation
description: Shared test utilities for real-filesystem, real-process testing. No mocks. Provides: temp dir helpers (auto-cleanup), OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR orchestration, plugin bootstrap harness, spawn capture helpers, binary-availability probes (to skip cleanly when sg/grep/find are absent), test fixture config files. Everything downstream depends on this.

title: E2E: test/e2e/runner.ts — plugin pipeline runner with greet banner
description:
## Objective
Build test/e2e/runner.ts exposing an exported async function runE2EScenario(opts): Promise<RunResult>. The runner is the core primitive every E2E scenario uses.

## Contract
- Loading target: imports the plugin via A3 (test/helpers/plugin-harness.ts) from **src/index.ts**. Do NOT load dist/index.js — no build precondition. If we later decide to exercise dist/, that is a separate bead.
- Failure behavior: runner is a library function. It NEVER calls process.exit. On step failure it returns RunResult { success: false, failureStepIndex, failureError }. Exit-code semantics for the test:e2e script live in ocb-3r3.15.2, not here.
- Determinism: when process.env.BYTES_TEST_DETERMINISTIC === '1', the logger (A5) strips timestamps and the runner uses a fixed runId (e.g. 'det-run') so NDJSON artifacts are byte-stable across runs.

## Flow
1. greet via A5 logger.greet — banner with plugin name 'oc-blackbytes', scenario name, runId, platform, node/bun version.
2. Create a temp OpenCode config dir via A1; copy the named fixture (A6) into '<dir>/oc-blackbytes.jsonc'; set OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR via A2 withEnv.
3. Load BlackbytesPlugin via A3 harness with { directory, worktree, client: TestClient }; capture the hooks object.
4. For each step in opts.steps: invoke the named hook with the step's input, capture output, write ONE NDJSON entry.
5. logger.summary on completion; return RunResult.

## NDJSON entry schema (pin exactly)
Each line is a JSON object with: { runId: string, scenario: string, stepIndex: number, stepName: string, hook: string, ts: string|null (null when deterministic), durationMs: number, ok: boolean, output?: unknown, error?: { message: string, name: string } }.

## RunResult shape (pin exactly)
{ runId: string, scenario: string, steps: Array<StepResult>, success: boolean, durationMs: number, artifactDir: string, failureStepIndex?: number, failureError?: { message: string; name: string } }

## Components
- test/e2e/runner.ts (new)
- test/e2e/types.ts (new) — RunResult, StepResult, RunE2EOptions
- test/e2e/artifacts/ (gitignored at repo root)

## Dependencies
A3 (plugin harness), A5 (logger), A6 (fixtures).

## DoD
- A smoke scenario (loads plugin, runs config hook, asserts hooks present) calls greet, logs 3+ steps, writes an NDJSON file at test/e2e/artifacts/{runId}/run.ndjson with one entry per step, and prints an 'E2E SUMMARY' block.
- Under BYTES_TEST_DETERMINISTIC=1, two consecutive runs produce byte-identical NDJSON.
- RunResult.success === false when any step throws; failureStepIndex + failureError populated.
- Runner never calls process.exit.
- Test script wiring lives in ocb-3r3.15.2, not this bead.

title: Wire test:e2e script + make 'bun run check' run E2E smoke
description:
## Objective
Wire package.json scripts so the unit vs E2E split is clean, and make the 'check' script run E2E smoke.

## Components
- package.json

## Dependencies
A3 plugin harness, M1 runner, M2 runner self-test.

## Required scripts
- 'test:unit' → 'bun test test/*.test.ts' (unit tests live at test/<name>.test.ts and test/<subdir>/*.test.ts EXCLUDING test/e2e/)
- 'test:e2e' → 'BYTES_TEST_DETERMINISTIC=1 bun test test/e2e/**/*.test.ts'
- Preserve existing 'test' as the unit loop (keep it fast). Update it to the same glob as 'test:unit' so 'bun test' does NOT pick up E2E.
- 'check' → 'biome check src test && bun run test:unit && bun run test:e2e' (keep lint+format as the first gate).

## Exit-code ownership
'test:e2e' relies on bun test's own exit code. The runner function (M1) never calls process.exit; the bun:test wrapper in M2 calls expect() on RunResult.success, so failing scenarios fail bun test, which exits non-zero.

## Out of scope
- Do NOT build dist/ before running E2E. M1 loads src/index.ts via A3.

## DoD
- 'bun run test:unit' runs all existing and new unit tests, does NOT execute any file under test/e2e/.
- 'bun run test:e2e' greets, runs the runner self-test (M2) + any wired scenarios, prints summary, exits 0 on success and non-zero on scenario failure.
- 'bun run check' runs biome, then unit, then e2e, in that order.

- Triggers: on push to any branch and on pull_request to default branch.
- Job: ubuntu-latest.
- Steps, in order: actions/checkout@v4; setup-bun (oven-sh/setup-bun@v2 with bun-version from package.json engines if set, else latest); bun install --frozen-lockfile (or bun install if no lockfile committed); bun run check; bun test --coverage; bun run test:e2e.
- Artifacts: upload test/e2e/artifacts/** on failure so NDJSON logs are inspectable.

- YAML parses (run 'yamllint' or equivalent if installed; otherwise 'bun run check' is sufficient since the file is not in src/test scope).

title: Unit: hashline-edit/hash-computation.ts
description:
## Objective
Test the two-letter CID hash (src/extensions/tools/hashline-edit/hash-computation.ts). Alphabet: ZPMQVRWSNKTXJBYH.

## Components
- src/extensions/tools/hashline-edit/hash-computation.ts
- test/hashline-edit/hash-computation.test.ts (new)

## Scope
IN:
- Determinism: same input (line content + line number or whichever inputs the function takes — read source first) always yields the same 2-char CID across back-to-back calls.
- Alphabet: output is always exactly 2 chars AND each char is in the set ZPMQVRWSNKTXJBYH.
- Distribution: pin at least 10 (input → output) pairs as a literal table so future changes to the algorithm are intentional.
- Edge inputs: empty string, line with only whitespace, very long line (≥2000 chars), unicode (emoji, Vietnamese diacritics), CRLF vs LF line endings (must or must-not affect the CID — pin behaviour observed in source).

## Risk note — cross-runtime drift
The project runs under Bun but tests may be re-run under Node via CI. If the algorithm uses any runtime-specific hashing (e.g. Bun's native hash), the snapshot table is fragile. Action: read hash-computation.ts BEFORE writing the snapshot. If it uses only pure JS (e.g. manual char math, sdbm, fnv), snapshot is safe. If it uses Bun.hash or crypto with specific digest truncation, ensure the test reproduces the exact same pipeline and add a comment in the test file explaining the dependency.

## DoD
- 10+ snapshot pairs asserted.
- All edge inputs asserted.
- Test passes identically on consecutive 'bun test' runs.
- Test file header documents any runtime assumption discovered while reading the source.
close_reason: test/hashline-edit/hash-computation.test.ts created: 31 tests covering computeLineHash, computeLegacyLineHash, formatHashLine, formatHashLines; alphabet validation, determinism, CRLF, unicode, edge inputs

A1 (tmp dir).

## Scope
IN scenarios:
- create-new-file: append/prepend to a nonexistent path produces a new file with the expected contents.
- delete-file: delete=true removes the file; subsequent read raises ENOENT.
- rename: rename moves final content to the new path and removes the old path.
- mixed-edits-one-call: multiple operations (replace + append + delete line) in a single call applied bottom-up produce the expected final file.
- failing edit: when one op in a multi-op call fails validation (e.g. anchor mismatch), assert WHOLE-CALL ATOMICITY — no partial writes land on disk. If the current executor is per-op atomic instead, document that explicitly in a comment and assert the per-op behavior, then open a follow-up bead for whole-call atomicity.

## Rollback contract
Pin in the test file header:
- 'A single executor call is atomic at the call level: either every operation applies to disk, or none does.' OR, if source differs, the actual observed contract documented in-test.

## DoD
- All five scenarios assert final on-disk contents via real reads.
- Failing-edit scenario asserts the file mtime and contents are unchanged from before the call.
- No mocks.

## Scope
IN:
- Normal exit within budget: spawn a tiny node one-liner ('node -e "console.log(1)"') → resolves with { stdout, stderr, exitCode: 0 }.

- Timeout kill: long-running node one-liner ('node -e "setInterval(()=>{},1000)"') with a small timeout (e.g. 200ms) → rejects or resolves with a clearly-flagged timeout result (match the source's contract — read it first). Child process is reaped (no zombies).

- On CI with tight timing, the 200ms timeout may be flaky — assert semantics (exitCode / timed-out flag) rather than exact durations; allow ±100ms jitter if you assert durationMs at all.

## DoD

- No zombie processes after the test (verify by spawning child, awaiting, then asserting pid is gone via process.kill(pid, 0) throwing ESRCH).

title: Unit: tools/shared/binary-downloader.ts (against a local HTTP server)
description:
## Objective
Test src/extensions/tools/shared/binary-downloader.ts against a real local HTTP server.

## Components
- src/extensions/tools/shared/binary-downloader.ts
- test/tools-shared/binary-downloader.test.ts (new)

## Dependencies
A1 (temp dir for download target).

## Important
- Per user directive: NO MOCKS. A real local node:http server is a real dependency, not a mock. Start the server in beforeAll, close in afterAll.

## Scope
IN scenarios:
- 200 success: server serves a small fixture with a known sha256; downloader writes the file; computed sha matches; file contents match byte-for-byte.
- 404: downloader rejects with an error whose message includes the HTTP status and URL.
- 500: same — error surfaces status.
- Truncated response: server writes part of the body then closes the socket before Content-Length bytes arrive. Downloader MUST:
  - detect truncation (Content-Length mismatch or stream-close-before-eof),
  - NOT leave a usable file at the target path (either no file, or a quarantined .tmp file that is NOT promoted),
  - reject with a clear error.
- Checksum mismatch (server responds 200 with correct length but wrong bytes): downloader MUST remove or quarantine the file and reject with a sha-mismatch error.

## Risk note — partial downloads
- This is the canonical place to pin partial-download semantics for the whole plugin. Whatever behavior source implements (atomic rename-on-success, quarantine, delete-on-fail), document and assert it precisely in the test file header.

## DoD
- 5 scenarios above asserted.
- Server port chosen dynamically (0 → get actual port) to avoid collisions.
- Server closed in afterAll even on failure.
- No leaked file descriptors (assert by trying to bind the same port after close).
close_reason: test/tools-shared/binary-downloader.test.ts: 5 tests — HTTP 200/404/500, truncated response, local server

title: Create workspace boundary utility
description:
**Objective:** Create a shared `assertWithinWorkspace(resolvedPath, workspaceRoot)` utility that validates any path is within the workspace root, resolving symlinks.

**Context:** Bundled tools (grep, glob, hashline-edit, ast-grep) receive user/agent-supplied paths with no validation against the workspace root. The workspace root (`directory`) is available in the tool execution context but never checked.

**Scope:**

**Assumptions and Constraints:**
- Use `realpath` to resolve symlinks before comparison
- For non-existent files (hashline-edit creates), validate the parent directory's realpath instead
- Handle edge cases: path equals workspace root (allowed for grep/glob), trailing slashes, double slashes

title: Gate hashline-edit prompt sections on config
description:
**Objective:** Only include hashline-edit workflow instructions in agent prompts when `hashline_edit` is enabled in config.

**Context:** Agent prompts (bytes default/gpt/gemini, general) unconditionally include `SHARED_SECTIONS.hashlineEditWorkflow`. When hashline_edit is disabled, these instructions are misleading.

**Scope:**
- IN: Make `SHARED_SECTIONS.hashlineEditWorkflow` inclusion conditional based on `hashline_edit` config
- IN: Thread `hashlineEditEnabled` boolean through agent factory functions
- IN: Affects `bytes/default.ts`, `bytes/gpt.ts`, `bytes/gemini.ts`, and `general` agent prompt
- OUT: Changing prompt content itself

**Implementation Approach:**
The agent factory functions (e.g., `createBytesAgent(model: string)` in `src/extensions/agents/bytes/index.ts`) need a second parameter: `hashlineEditEnabled: boolean`. The factories call prompt builders (e.g., `createDefaultBytesPrompt` in `bytes/default.ts`) which use template literals with `SHARED_SECTIONS.hashlineEditWorkflow` at specific line positions (e.g., line ~63 in default.ts). Replace unconditional `${SHARED_SECTIONS.hashlineEditWorkflow}` with a conditional: `${hashlineEditEnabled ? SHARED_SECTIONS.hashlineEditWorkflow : ""}`. The caller in `agent-config-handler.ts` (which calls the factories) has access to the plugin config and can pass `pluginConfig.hashline_edit !== false`.

**Components Touched:**
- `src/extensions/agents/bytes/default.ts` — conditional inclusion at line ~63
- `src/extensions/agents/bytes/gpt.ts` — similar conditional
- `src/extensions/agents/bytes/gemini.ts` — similar conditional
- `src/extensions/agents/bytes/index.ts` — `createBytesAgent(model, hashlineEditEnabled)` signature change
- `src/extensions/agents/general.ts` — if it references hashline-edit section
- `src/handlers/config-handler/agent-config-handler.ts` — pass `hashlineEditEnabled` when calling factories

**Dependencies:** None

**Assumptions and Constraints:**
- `hashline_edit` defaults to true (enabled) — prompt content unchanged for default config
- When disabled, omit the entire hashline-edit workflow section, do not leave empty markers
- Ensure no double blank lines from conditional omission (use trim or filter)

**Validation:**
- `bun run build` succeeds
- `bun run check` passes
- With hashline_edit disabled: agent prompts do NOT contain hashline workflow text
- With hashline_edit enabled (default): agent prompts are unchanged
close_reason: Hashline-edit prompt sections gated on hashlineEditEnabled param
